reasons: Baxia directly reduces Alice's HP regeneration and sustain value.

proof.summary: Alice depends heavily on HP regeneration and long sustain fights, while Baxia directly reduces enemy shield and HP regen effects.
worksBestWhen: Baxia applies his skills during Alice's sustain window.
Baxia stays close enough to keep pressure on Alice.
Team follows up while Alice's healing is reduced.
failureCases: Alice avoids Baxia and dives the backline.
Baxia's team does not follow up.

reasons: Karrie can repeatedly damage Alice's high-HP sustain build with Max HP-based true damage.

proof.summary: Alice becomes durable through HP and sustain, while Karrie can repeatedly deal Max HP-based true damage from safe distance.
worksBestWhen: Team applies anti-heal or crowd control together with Karrie's DPS.

reasons: Lunox can burst durable heroes with magic penetration before Alice drains a long fight.

proof.summary: Alice wants extended drain fights, while Lunox can use high magic penetration and burst to damage durable heroes before sustain takes over.
worksBestWhen: Lunox bursts Alice before long sustain happens.
Alice is forced to commit forward.
failureCases: Lunox is caught after using escape.

reasons: Alpha's repeated true damage can pressure Alice's durability in extended fights.

proof.summary: Alice is durable in long fights, while Alpha can repeatedly trigger true damage to pressure durable heroes and contest sustain with support.
worksBestWhen: Alpha repeatedly triggers Beta true damage.
Alpha fights with anti-heal support or item.
Alice is controlled or slowed during the fight.
failureCases: Alice fights with multiple targets around her.

reasons: Valir can push, slow, and disengage Alice before she drains grouped enemies.

proof.summary: Alice wants to stay close and drain grouped enemies, while Valir can push, slow, and disengage her from the teamfight.
worksBestWhen: Valir saves knockback for Alice's dive.
Team spreads out instead of grouping.
